title: Bipolar & Lithium Mastery
content:
• Dx: Manic Episode: Sleeping little (>1wk), pressured speech.
• Risk: Lithium Toxicity: Precipitated by Advil (NSAIDs). Use Aspirin.
• Tx: Dialysis if Level >4.

title: Personality Disorders
content:
• Cluster A (Weird): Paranoid (Accusatory), Schizoid (Aloof).
• Cluster B (Wild): Antisocial (Bad), Borderline (Instability), Histrionic.
• Cluster C (Worried): Avoidant, OCPD (Compulsive).

title: APGAR & Birth Injuries
content:
• Dx: APGAR: 1 min (tolerance), 5 min (resuscitation). NOT a neuro predictor.
• Dx: Erb-Duchenne (C5-C6): Waiters tip position.
• Tx: Clavicular Fracture: No tx needed.

title: Celiac Disease Basics
content:
• Dx: Diarrhea + abdominal distention in thin child.
• Tx: Strict gluten-free diet.

title: Consent & Refusal
content:
• Rule: Do not override capable adult refusal (e.g. Jehovah Witness).
• Rule: Mature Minor is capacity-based in Canada.

title: Dermatology High-Yield
content:
• Dx: Tinea versicolor: Hypopigmented patches (Malassezia furfur).
• Tx: Scabies: Permethrin cream for patient and family.
• Dx: Tinea Capitis: Broken hairs in localized hair loss.

title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score | Stroke Risk in AFib
content:
• Dx: Used to determine the need for Anticoagulation in Atrial Fibrillation.
1 Point: CHF, HTN, DM, Vascular disease, Age 65-74, Female.
2 Points: Age ≥75, Prior Stroke/TIA/Thrombo-embolism.
• Tx: Score ≥2 (Male) or ≥3 (Female) → Start Warfarin or DOAC (Apixaban/Rivaroxaban).
• Risk: Do not count age twice (if 76, you get 2 pts, not 3).

title: Centor Score | Strep Pharyngitis Management
content:
• Dx: Criteria to treat sore throat with antibiotics.
Cough?NONE (1 pt)
Exudates?YES (1 pt)
Nodes?Tender Ant. Cervical (1 pt)
Temperature?>38°C (1 pt)
• Tx: Score 4+ → Treat with Penicillin immediately.
• Tx: Score 2-3 → Rapid Strep Test first.

title: Master Screening Summary (MCCQE1 High-Yield)
content: • Colon Cancer: Age 50-75 (Colonoscopy q10y). If relative <60: Start @40 or 10y before. • Breast Cancer: Mammogram starts @40 (Yearly). • Cervical Cancer: Pap Smear Ages 21-65 (q3y). • Lung Cancer: Age 55 (Low-dose CT) if 30 pack-year smoker. • AAA: Age 65 (One-time US) for any male smoker. • DEXA Scan: Starts @65 for Osteoporosis screening.

title: Antibiotic Pearls & LAME Rule
content:
• Warning: Remember the LAME rule for Cephalosporins (They do NOT cover):
L - Listeria
A - Atypical PNA (Mycoplasma/Chlamydia)
M - MRSA (Except Ceftaroline)
E - Enterococcus
